en: A false balance is an abomination to the LORD, but a just weight is his delight.
zh: 詭詐的天平是耶和華所厭惡的， 準確的法碼是他所喜悅的。

en: When pride comes, then comes disgrace, but with the humble is wisdom.
zh: 傲慢來，羞辱也來； 謙卑的人卻有智慧。

en: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them.
zh: 正直人的純正必引導他們自己， 奸詐人的奸惡卻毀滅自己。

en: Riches do not profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.
zh: 在 神發怒的日子，財物毫無益處； 唯有公義能救人脫離死亡。

en: The righteousness of the blameless keeps his way straight, but the wicked falls by his own wickedness.
zh: 完全人的公義，必使自己的路平坦正直， 但惡人必因自己的邪惡跌倒。

en: The righteousness of the upright delivers them, but the treacherous are taken captive by their lust.
zh: 正直人的公義必拯救自己， 但奸詐人必陷溺於自己的惡慾中。

en: When the wicked dies, his hope will perish, and the expectation of wealth perishes too.
zh: 惡人一死，他的希望就幻滅； 有能力的人的盼望也消滅了。

en: The righteous is delivered from trouble, and the wicked walks into it instead.
zh: 義人得蒙拯救脫離患難， 惡人卻來代替他。

en: With his mouth the godless man would destroy his neighbor, but by knowledge the righteous are delivered.
zh: 不敬虔的人用口敗壞鄰舍， 義人卻因知識免受其害。

en: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices, and when the wicked perish there are shouts of gladness.
zh: 義人亨通，全城歡樂； 惡人滅亡，大家歡呼。

en: By the blessing of the upright a city is exalted, but by the mouth of the wicked it is overthrown.
zh: 因正直人的祝福，城的地位就提高； 因惡人的口，城就傾覆。

en: Whoever belittles his neighbor lacks sense, but a man of understanding remains silent.
zh: 藐視鄰舍的，真是無知； 聰明人卻緘默不言。

en: Whoever goes about slandering reveals secrets, but he who is trustworthy in spirit keeps a thing covered.
zh: 到處搬弄是非的，洩露祕密， 心裡誠實的，遮隱事情。

en: Where there is no guidance, a people falls, but in an abundance of counselors there is safety.
zh: 沒有智謀，國家敗落； 謀士眾多，就能得勝。

en: Whoever puts up security for a stranger will surely suffer harm, but he who hates striking hands in pledge is secure.
zh: 為外人作保證人的，必受虧損； 厭惡替人擊掌擔保的，卻得著安穩。

en: A gracious woman gets honor, and violent men get riches.
zh: 賢德的婦女得著尊榮， 強暴的男子只得著財富。

en: A man who is kind benefits himself, but a cruel man hurts himself.
zh: 仁慈的人自己獲益， 殘忍的人自己受害。

en: The wicked earns deceptive wages, but one who sows righteousness gets a sure reward.
zh: 惡人賺得的工價是虛假的， 播種公義的得著實在的賞賜。

en: Whoever is steadfast in righteousness will live, but he who pursues evil will die.
zh: 恆心行義的必得生命； 追隨邪惡的必致死亡。

en: Those of crooked heart are an abomination to the LORD, but those of blameless ways are his delight.
zh: 欺詐的心是耶和華厭惡的； 行為完全是他所喜悅的。

en: Be assured, an evil person will not go unpunished, but the offspring of the righteous will be delivered.
zh: 惡人必不免受罰， 但義人的後裔必蒙解救。

en: Like a gold ring in a pig's snout is a beautiful woman without discretion.
zh: 婦女美麗而無見識， 就像金環帶在豬鼻上一樣。

en: The desire of the righteous ends only in good; the expectation of the wicked in wrath.
zh: 義人的願望，盡是美好， 惡人的希望，必招致忿怒。

en: One gives freely, yet grows all the richer; another withholds what he should give, and only suffers want.
zh: 有人慷慨好施，財富卻更增添； 有人吝嗇過度，反招致貧窮。

en: Whoever brings blessing will be enriched, and one who waters will himself be watered.
zh: 樂善好施的人，必得豐裕； 施惠於人的，自己也必蒙施惠。

en: The people curse him who holds back grain, but a blessing is on the head of him who sells it.
zh: 屯積五穀的，必被人民咒詛； 出售糧食的，福祉必臨到他的頭上。

en: Whoever diligently seeks good seeks favor, but evil comes to him who searches for it.
zh: 殷切求善的，必得到愛顧； 追求邪惡的，邪惡必臨到他。

en: Whoever trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous will flourish like a green leaf.
zh: 倚賴自己財富的，必然衰落； 義人卻必繁茂，好像綠葉。

en: Whoever troubles his own household will inherit the wind, and the fool will be servant to the wise of heart.
zh: 禍害自己家庭的，必承受清風； 愚妄人必作心思智慧的人的僕人。

en: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, and whoever captures souls is wise.
zh: 義人所結的果子就是生命樹； 智慧人必能得人。

en: If the righteous is repaid on earth, how much more the wicked and the sinner!
zh: 義人在世上尚且受報應， 何況惡人和罪人呢？
